params.name: Ancient Compass
params.description:
On May 8, 356 BC, the first compass was created by a person from Zhao, located on the magnetic hill in Hebei, China.
The compass was an ancient instrument used in China to identify directions and is the invention of the ancient Chinese people who had a long-term understanding of magnetic objects. The earliest compass was made of a natural magnet stone, shaped like a spoon, and placed on a smooth plate, utilizing the guiding function of a magnetic needle to identify direction.
